route: ROUTE 3 (monotone escape / transience) via the SPOOL COORDINATE

spool_coordinate.exact_identity: Q(Mp) = 9 Q(p)  =>  u(Mp) = u(p) + 1   (verified exact on 1e5 random integer vectors)
spool_coordinate.companion_angle: theta(p) = polar angle of (y,z) in the Q_yz-orthonormal (Cholesky) frame; under M advances by alpha = arccos(-1/6) (irrational)
spool_coordinate.derivation: Q is the invariant quadratic form of the modulus-3 map M = ((3,0,0),(0,0,-3),(0,3,-1)); each M application pays out exactly one spool turn (+1 in u).

transience_measured.interpretation: min_i [u(p_{i+n}) - u(p_i)] >= -b with b ~ 0.5 TURN, level-stable/shrinking. The walk never reels the spool back more than ~half a turn. CONTRAST: the plain x-coordinate had floor -2n (linear, useless). So WEAK TRANSIENCE HOLDS in u.

what_the_spool_PROVES_cleanly.meaning: Because u advances +1 per M with bounded backslide, and deep-tail separation gives min|M^j v|_inf = 3/8/24, the birth levels meeting any Cheb-<=10 ball are confined to a band of <=4. This EXCLUDES cross-scale stacking (the only thing that could blow A0 up as k->infinity). Non-circular: uses only the u-identity + the proven deep-tail bound, never counts the crowd.

where_the_spool_STOPS.in_shell_flatness: For every dense ball, the arcs are NOT separated by u (min_interarc_u_sep = 0.0) NOR by theta (spread < 0.01 rad). Same-shell returns are invisible to the spiral coordinate.

crowding_is_a_single_local_window.meaning: ALL of c(q,r) for r<=10 lives in ONE bounded path-window (span <=70, level-uniform); genuine far-returns (gap > 2x ballistic sojourn) essentially never stack (<=1). The <=5 arcs are LOCAL in/out meander of a single pass, dominated by birth-level-0 fresh connectors around one anchor.

A0.measured: A(q,r) <= 5 for r in [1,10], level-stable L5/L6/L7
A0.proven_component: far-return arcs <= birthspread <= 4 (spool + deep-tail, level-uniform, non-circular)
A0.residual_component: local same-shell meander arcs, bounded by the local-window span <=~70, a LEVEL-UNIFORM but not-yet-proven fact

residual_lemma: LOCAL-WINDOW / SAME-SHELL NO-RETURN: the path-index span of the walk points inside any Cheb-<=10 ball is bounded by a level-independent constant W0 (~70). Equivalently: within a single shell the walk does not re-approach itself (Cheb<=10) except inside a bounded path-window. The spool/rotation is FLAT in-shell so transience does NOT prove this; it reduces to a FIXED-SCALE finite check on the self-similar connector-word library (length<=5 alphabet), NOT the unbounded k-induction.
non_circular_verdict: The delivered (cross-scale) bound is non-circular and collapses the k-induction to a base check. The residual is the anti-stacking residue localized to one shell + fixed finite alphabet -- same TYPE as the parent, but at bounded scale, so it is a finite computation rather than the unbounded parent crowding.
